projects.raw_text:
Designing of L1-L2 Cache Memory

Jul 2020 – Present


Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi

Project aims to reduce the power of reference memory compiler by 50 percent. Critical path has been studied to estimate PPA impact along with literature survey done to analyze different power saving schemes without affecting performance. I am working on L1 Cache. Hierarchical bitline architecture is proposed for both L1 and L2 Cache and implemented to reduce dynamic read power. Local bit line is coupled with global bit line and thorough analysis and comparison between two schemes, gate coupled and direct coupled is done.

Design and Benchmark a library of  Low voltage , High Density  6-T SRAM cells 

Feb 2020 – May 2020

Designed four 6-T SRAM cells at low voltage having different read/write 
assist schemes associated with them. Cells had been benchmarked using 
various Figures of Merit along with the designing of high density Layouts. 
Analyzed the variations in Figures of Merit with six sigma qualification by 
running Monte-Carlo simulations in Eldo.

RTL to GDS Flow

Feb 2020 – May 2020

A digital circuit was designed using various CAD tools. The project 
involves requirement gathering, analysis and RTL design till writing
into the GDS file. Tools like Synopsys VCS for Simulation and Code 
Coverage, Tempus for STA, RTL compiler for Logic Synthesis, Conformal 
for Logical Equivalence Checking and Cadence Encounter for Physical 
design were used. Scan chain based Design was employed for DFT. 
Various parameters affecting the PPA were analyzed and optimum design 
was presented.

Design & Analysis of OAI221 Complex Static Circuit (CADENCE VIRTUOSO 65nm)      

Sep 2019 – Nov 2019

Designed a OAI221 static circuit using Cadence Virtuoso in 65nm 
technology. Circuit was simulated to measure power, performance 
and area, followed by a dense Layout. Post-layout simulations were 
done using Mentor Graphics Eldo.

Hybrid Memory Cube (HMC) Model Implementation

Sep 2019 – Nov 2019

Analyzing the effect of HMC model on system performance is done  by running different benchmarks on Gem5. Performance is compared with  DRAM memory.
